title: Light Kenyan Rice Flour Pancakes
description: A traditional Kenyan dish featuring fermented rice flour pancakes, perfect for a light and indulgent breakfast or snack. These gluten-free friendly pancakes are slow-cooked to perfection.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine rice flour, yeast, sugar, and salt.
Gradually add water to the bowl, mixing until a smooth batter forms.
Cover the bowl with a cloth and let it ferment in a warm place for 2 hours.
After fermentation, add coconut milk, eggs, and melted butter to the batter, mixing well.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter onto the pan.
Cook the pancakes for 2-3 minutes, until bubbles appear on the surface and the edges start to dry.
Flip the pancakes and cook for another 1-2 minutes, until golden brown.
Serve the pancakes hot with your favorite toppings.
Repeat the cooking process until all the batter is used up.
